stem: A agriculture study measures the yield per hectare (tonnes) for a sample of 15 farms. The sample mean is 167.8 tonnes with a sample standard deviation of 32.7 tonnes. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 161.1 tonnes and standard deviation of 22.8 tonnes. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 167.8, 95% CI = [149.7, 185.9]. Without outliers: mean = 161.1, 95% CI = [147.9, 174.3]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [147.9, 185.9]. Point estimate: 164.4 tonnes.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [147.9, 185.9].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
